normalize: P1: No design token for focus ring — focus-visible styles not visually confirmed; skip-link exists but keyboard navigation focus states need verification
P2: Three font families loaded (Instrument Serif, Schibsted Grotesk, DM Mono) — good intent but no preload hints for any of them, risking FOIT
P2: CSS custom properties exist (--bg, --blue, --text, etc.) but no documented design system or token file — ad-hoc usage
P2: 7 inline styles detected — should be moved to stylesheet for consistency
harden: P0: All 3 GitHub Marketplace links (marketplace, documentation, agents link) return HTTP 404 — primary conversion path is completely broken
P1: Issues link uses mailto:support@rafayels.engineering — if email is not set up, users get a dead end with no feedback
P2: No error state handling for copy-to-clipboard buttons if clipboard API is unavailable
P2: No 404 page or error boundary evident for the SPA
optimize: P1: No font preload hints — 3 Google Fonts families loaded without preconnect optimization (preconnect exists but no preload for critical font)
P2: No deferred or async scripts detected — all JS appears render-blocking
P2: No lazy loading — all 276 DOM elements load immediately (though page is relatively light)
P3: 0 images on the page — entirely text-based, which is lightweight but visually sparse
adapt: P1: Dark mode only — no light mode or color scheme toggle; users with light mode preference get no accommodation
P1: No prefers-reduced-motion handling detected — scroll-triggered animations may affect motion-sensitive users
P2: No prefers-contrast handling for high-contrast mode users
P2: Page tested only at 1280px viewport — mobile responsiveness not visually verified but nav has toggle button suggesting mobile support exists
clarify: P1: 'Changelog Soon' in footer is vague — no date, no link to roadmap
P2: Hero subtitle 'Ship features end-to-end in Claude Code' — could be more specific about what 'end-to-end' means for someone unfamiliar
P2: '/re:feature' button in hero section — unclear what this does without context; no tooltip or explanation
P2: '29 Agents, 22 Skills, 19 Commands' stats in hero — impressive numbers but no context for what agents/skills/commands mean to a new user

findings: P1: CTA 'Install Plugin' button hover state shows subtle brightening but the change is minimal — low affordance signal at screenshot resolution (/polish)
P1: Nav links show hover state with color shift but very subtle — could benefit from underline or more pronounced color change (/delight)
P2: Secondary 'See How It Works' link hover barely distinguishable from default state (/polish)
P2: '/re:feature' button hover appears to have a border glow effect — good but inconsistent with other button styles (/normalize)
P2: Footer links hover states not verified to be distinct enough from body text color (/polish)

findings.description: All 3 external GitHub Marketplace links return HTTP 404 — the primary conversion/install path is completely broken
findings.recommendation: Fix the marketplace URL to point to the actual published listing, or set up a redirect. If not yet published, use a pre-launch landing page or GitHub repo link instead

findings.description: Page is entirely text-based with zero images, illustrations, or visual media — the 'Built for Serious Work' and agents sections feel like walls of text
findings.recommendation: Add architectural diagrams, agent workflow visualizations, or at minimum SVG icons for feature cards to break up text density

findings.description: Dark developer-tool landing page with blue accents follows the most common SaaS template pattern — indistinguishable from hundreds of similar pages
findings.recommendation: Introduce a distinctive visual element: the engineering/blueprint metaphor could use technical drawing aesthetics, grid overlays, or the 'A' logo motif as a repeated design element

findings.description: Feature pipeline section (5 columns: Brainstorm, Plan, Work, Review, Learn) has very dense text in each column with small font — hard to scan at a glance
findings.recommendation: Use icons or numbers as visual anchors for each step, increase spacing between columns, and trim descriptions to 1-2 lines max

findings.description: H1 at 96px is extremely large relative to body text at 18px — the 5.3:1 ratio creates jarring scale jumps. H2 at 56px is also very large
findings.recommendation: Consider a more modular scale: H1 ~64px, H2 ~40px, H3 ~28px for smoother visual hierarchy transitions

findings.description: Dark-mode-only design with no light mode alternative excludes users who prefer or need light backgrounds (accessibility concern)
findings.recommendation: Add a prefers-color-scheme media query with light mode palette, or at minimum a manual theme toggle

findings.description: No prefers-reduced-motion query — scroll-triggered reveal animations (.reveal.visible) will play for motion-sensitive users
findings.recommendation: Wrap all animations in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) and provide instant-visible fallback

findings.description: The '/re:feature' button in the hero is jargon — new users won't know this is a CLI command without context
findings.recommendation: Add a tooltip or inline label explaining this is the main CLI command, e.g., 'Run this in your terminal'

findings.description: Font loading uses Google Fonts with preconnect but no preload — first paint likely shows FOIT or FOUT for 3 font families
findings.recommendation: Add <link rel='preload'> for the critical font (Schibsted Grotesk regular) and use font-display: swap explicitly

findings.description: No visible loading states or skeleton screens — if the page loads slowly, users see nothing until fonts resolve
findings.recommendation: Add a minimal CSS-only loading state and ensure critical content renders before web fonts

findings.description: 29 Specialized Agents section mentions agents but only shows a truncated list with a 'view all' link (that 404s) — the actual agent list content is unclear
findings.recommendation: Show a curated grid of 6-8 key agents with icons and one-line descriptions, with the rest accessible via working link

findings.description: Three distinct font families creates visual complexity — Instrument Serif for headings, Schibsted Grotesk for body, DM Mono for code. The serif/sans-serif contrast is good but the monospace font adds a third axis
findings.recommendation: Consider whether DM Mono could be replaced with Schibsted Grotesk in a different weight for non-code UI elements to reduce font load

findings.description: Body text color rgb(200, 214, 229) on background rgb(10, 14, 23) gives approximately 11:1 contrast — good. But the dim text variant (--text-dim) may fall below 4.5:1 on the dark background
findings.recommendation: Verify --text-dim meets WCAG AA (4.5:1) for all usage contexts; if used for descriptive text, ensure it's at least rgb(150, 160, 170)

findings.description: Hover states are present but inconsistently applied — CTA buttons have subtle brightening while nav links have color shifts, creating an uneven interaction pattern
findings.recommendation: Standardize hover behavior: all buttons get a consistent brightness/shadow lift, all text links get consistent underline or color shift

findings.description: Terminal/code block demo in workflow section is a static display — no interactivity, typing animation, or copy feedback beyond a button
findings.recommendation: Add a typing animation to the terminal demo that shows the actual /re:feature command running with live output, creating a memorable demo moment

findings.description: Footer 'Changelog Soon' span provides no actionable information — no subscribe option, no timeline
findings.recommendation: Either remove it or add a 'Notify me' email capture to turn this into a lead generation opportunity

findings.description: 7 inline styles detected in the DOM — minor consistency issue
findings.recommendation: Move all inline styles to the stylesheet for maintainability

summary: A competent dark-mode developer tool landing page with solid semantic HTML (proper ARIA labels, skip link, landmark regions, single H1) and a clear information hierarchy — but severely undermined by all external links returning 404, making the entire conversion funnel broken. The visual design is functional but generic: dark background with blue accents is the most overused pattern in dev tooling. The page needs visual media (zero images), better hover consistency, reduced-motion support, and a light mode option. Typography choices are intentional but the extreme scale jumps (96px H1 vs 18px body) feel unresolved. Fix the broken links first, then invest in visual differentiation and interaction polish.
